info.title: WID 3.0 API — National Draft
info.description:
Working draft of the national **Workforce Information Database (WID) 3.0** API specification.
This specification is being developed collaboratively by multiple state workforce agencies
under the Analyst Resource Center (ARC) consortium.

## Design philosophy

1. **Table-native endpoints** — every WID 3.0 core table is exposed as a queryable resource
   at its own path with filter parameters that mirror the table's columns exactly.
2. **View endpoints** — cross-table views join related tables and return enriched records
   (e.g., CES employment with resolved geography names and series titles).
3. **Consistent naming** — all API field names use `camelCase` equivalents of the WID 3.0
   column names (e.g., `StFips` → `stFips`, `AreaTypeVersion` → `areaTypeVersion`).
4. **Standard envelope** — every list response wraps results in `{ meta, data, links }`.
   The envelope supports both page-number and cursor pagination.
5. **State-agnostic** — `stFips` is never hardcoded; the spec supports all 50 states plus
   national data.

## Key structural changes from WID 2.8 / Oregon reference spec

- `AreaTypeVersion` is a new field in WID 3.0 that is part of the geography primary key.
  It allows historical vintages of area definitions (especially MSAs) to coexist.
- CES is a **single table** containing employment, production-worker stats, and
  hours/earnings.  The Oregon spec split these into separate endpoints, which is a 2.8-era
  design choice not needed in 3.0.
- `SeriesCodeType` is part of the CES primary key alongside `SeriesCode` (to support
  multiple industry classification systems).
- Lookup tables such as `IndDirectories` and `OccDirectories` now serve as the canonical
  directory of projection-period matrix codes, replacing the ad-hoc listIndustries /
  listOccupations sub-endpoints in the Oregon spec.
- Licensing tables (`License`, `LicenseAuthorities`, `LicenseHistory`, `LicenseXOcc`)
  are new in WID 3.0 with no equivalent in WID 2.8.

## Reference documents

- [WID 3.0 Structure (Nov 2025)](../WID-3.0-Structure-20251120.pdf)
- [WID v3.0 Addenda](../WID-v30-Addenda.pdf)
- [Oregon WID 2.8 reference spec](../../reference/oregon-wid-2.8.yaml)

For background on the WID standard see https://widcenter.org.
This is a living document — open a pull request to propose changes.

servers.description: Placeholder — update with actual endpoint URL before testing

tags.name: lookups
tags.description: Reference and lookup tables — stable code lists and geographic registries. These correspond 1-to-1 with WID 3.0 lookup tables.

tags.name: ces
tags.description: Current Employment Statistics (CES) — employment, hours, and earnings estimates by industry series, area, and period.  Maps to the WID 3.0 `CES` table.

tags.name: laborForce
tags.description: Local Area Unemployment Statistics (LAUS) — civilian labor force, employment, unemployment, and rates by area and period.  Maps to the WID 3.0 `LaborForce` table.

tags.name: industry
tags.description: QCEW industry employment and wage data by ownership type. Maps to the WID 3.0 `Industry` table.

tags.name: projections
tags.description: Industry-occupation employment projections and related directories. Maps to `ProjectionsMatrix`, `IndDirectories`, and `OccDirectories`.

tags.name: wages
tags.description: Occupation/industry wage data from OES/OEWS and other sources. Maps to the WID 3.0 `IOWage` table.

tags.name: licensing
tags.description: Occupational licensing data — new in WID 3.0, no equivalent in WID 2.8. Maps to `LicenseAuthorities`, `License`, `LicenseHistory`, and `LicenseXOcc`.

tags.name: views
tags.description: Pre-joined views that combine data across related WID 3.0 tables for common display patterns such as CES with geography names resolved.

tags.name: operational
tags.description: Health check and data availability endpoints.  Use `/health` to verify the service is reachable and `/status` to inspect which tables are loaded and how current the data is.

get.description: Returns geographic area descriptor records from the WID 3.0 `Geographies` table. The composite primary key is (stFips, areaType, areaTypeVersion, area). `areaTypeVersion` is new in WID 3.0 and allows multiple vintage definitions of the same area type (e.g., MSA 2013 vs MSA 2018 definitions) to coexist.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.summary: Get a single geography record by composite primary key

get.description: Area types include: '00'=National, '01'=State, '04'=County, '21'=MSA, '31'=Metropolitan Statistical Area (current standard), etc.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.description: Returns the list of valid state FIPS codes and their corresponding state names. The record with `stFips = '00'` represents the United States as a whole (national data) and is included in this table for completeness, though it is not a state. Use `stFips = '00'` on data endpoints to retrieve national-level aggregates.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.description: Returns the set of valid data years by periodType. Use this to determine what years are available for a given data series.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.description: Returns available time periods (periodType + period combinations). Replaces the per-domain `listYears`, `maxPeriod`, and `minPeriod` sub-endpoints found in the Oregon WID 2.8 spec — callers simply filter and sort here instead.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.description: Multi-code industry classification table. Covers NAICS (codeType='10'), SIC (codeType='05'), and any other classification systems a state uses.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.description: Multi-code occupation classification table. Covers SOC 2018 (codeType='19'), SOC 2010 (codeType='14'), OES (codeType='45'), and other systems.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.description: Returns CES industry series codes. `stFips` was added to this table as part of the **May 2025 WID 3.0 addenda** because there are genuine definitional differences between some CES series codes at the national vs. state level. Filter by `stFips` to retrieve state-specific series; omit it for national series.

parameters.name: seriesCodeType
parameters.description: Industry code classification type used for CES series

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.description: Ownership codes identify employer type. Common values: '10'=Total, '20'=Federal Government, '50'=Private.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.description: Rate type distinguishes hourly (rateType='1') from annual (rateType='2') wages.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.description: State-specific 2-character codes describing industry or occupation growth.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.description:
Returns employment estimates from the Current Employment Statistics program. The WID 3.0 CES table is a single table that contains employment, production-worker employment, hours, and earnings data — unlike the Oregon WID 2.8 spec which exposed these as separate endpoints.
Each record is uniquely identified by (stFips, areaType, areaTypeVersion, area, periodYear, periodType, period, seriesCodeType, seriesCode, adjusted).
Use response fields beginning with `supp` to determine whether specific sub-measures should be displayed.

parameters.name: seriesCodeType
parameters.description: Industry code type for CES series (e.g. '10' for NAICS)

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.description: Returns civilian labor force, employment, unemployment, and rate estimates from the Local Area Unemployment Statistics (LAUS) program. Primary key: (stFips, areaType, areaTypeVersion, area, periodYear, periodType, period, adjusted).

parameters.name: adjusted
parameters.description: '1' for seasonally adjusted, '0' for not adjusted

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.description: Returns covered employment and wage data from the ES-202/QCEW report. Each record covers one state, area, industry, ownership type, and time period. Monthly employment is stored as `month1Emp`, `month2Emp`, `month3Emp` (i.e., the three months of the quarter, or months 1/2/3 for annual records).

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.description: Returns industry-occupation employment projections. Primary key: (stFips, areaType, areaTypeVersion, area, periodYear, periodType, period, matrixIndCode, matrixOccCode). `projectedYear` indicates the target year. The `periodYear`/`periodType`/`period` keys identify the base-year projection run.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.summary: Query IndDirectories — matrix industry code directory
get.description: Returns the directory of MicroMatrix industry codes valid for a given projection run (periodYear / periodType / period / projectedYear). Use this to enumerate available industry codes before querying `/projections`. Replaces the Oregon-style `ioMatrix/listIndustries` sub-endpoint.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.summary: Query OccDirectories — matrix occupation code directory
get.description: Returns the directory of MicroMatrix occupation codes valid for a given projection run.  Replaces the Oregon-style `ioMatrix/listOccupations` sub-endpoint.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.description: Crosswalk from matrix industry codes to standard IndustryCodes entries.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.description: Crosswalk from matrix occupation codes to standard OccupationCodes entries.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.description: Returns occupational wage data from OES/OEWS and other wage programs. Each record covers one state, area, industry, occupation, wage source, rate type, and time period. Use `suppressWage`, `suppressAll`, and `suppressEmp` flags to determine whether wage or employment values may be displayed.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.description: Returns occupational licensing authority records. New in WID 3.0. Primary key: (stFips, areaType, areaTypeVersion, area, licAuthID).

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.description: Returns individual licenses authorized by a state. Primary key: (stFips, licenseID).

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.description: Returns the count of licenses awarded for a specific license, area, and period.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.description: Returns the crosswalk from licenses to standard OccupationCodes entries.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.summary: CES employment enriched with geography name and series title
get.description: Joins `CES` → `Geographies` → `CESCodes` to add `areaName` and `seriesTitle` to each CES record.  The most common display format for CES dashboards.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.summary: Labor force data enriched with geography name
get.description: Joins `LaborForce` → `Geographies` to add `areaName` to LAUS records.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.summary: Wage data enriched with occupation title, industry title, and area name
get.description: Joins `IOWage` → `OccupationCodes` → `IndustryCodes` → `Geographies` → `WageSources` → `WageRateTypes` to produce a display-ready wage record.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.summary: Projections enriched with industry title, occupation title, and area name
get.description: Joins `ProjectionsMatrix` → `IndDirectories` → `OccDirectories` → `Geographies` → `GrowthCodes` to produce display-ready projection records.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.summary: QCEW industry data enriched with area name, industry title, and ownership name
get.description: Joins `Industry` → `Geographies` → `IndustryCodes` → `Ownerships`.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.description: Joins `LicenseXOcc` → `License` → `LicenseAuthorities` → `OccupationCodes` to show which licenses are required for a given occupation in a given state.

schema.description: Flat CSV with one header row followed by one data row per record. Contains the same fields as the JSON `data` array items, in the same field order.  No metadata envelope or links are included. All string values are enclosed in double quotes.

get.description: Returns a minimal response confirming that the API service is reachable and operational.  Suitable for use by load balancers, uptime monitors, and CI smoke tests.  Does **not** verify database connectivity — use `/status` for that.

Returns a structured summary of which WID 3.0 tables have been loaded, for which states, and how current the data is.  Intended for administrators and data consumers who need to verify data currency before running reports.
Each entry in the `tables` array describes one table-by-state combination. The `dataAsOf` field is the date through which data has been loaded for that combination.  A `null` value means the table has not been loaded for that state.
This endpoint is state-agnostic: it returns information for all states that have data in the system.

page.name: page
page.description: Page number (1-based); ignored when `cursor` is supplied

pageSize.name: pageSize
pageSize.description: Records per page for page-number mode

cursor.name: cursor
cursor.description: Opaque cursor token for cursor-based pagination. If provided, implementations should ignore `page` and continue from the encoded position.

limit.name: limit
limit.description: Record cap for cursor-based pagination. If omitted, implementations may use `pageSize` as the effective limit.

stFips.name: stFips
stFips.description: 2-digit state FIPS code. Use '00' for national data. All 50 states are supported — this parameter is never restricted to a single state.

areaType.name: areaType
areaType.description: 2-digit area type code. Common values: '00'=National, '01'=State, '04'=County, '31'=MSA. Full list in `/lookups/areaTypes`.

areaTypeVersion.name: areaTypeVersion
areaTypeVersion.description:
Area type version — new field in WID 3.0. Allows historical vintage definitions of area types (especially MSAs) to coexist with current definitions. Version '0' represents the current definition.
**If omitted, no filter is applied on this field** — records across all versions are returned. This is intentional: requiring callers to know the correct version upfront would be a poor default experience, and the most common case (fetching current data) is achieved by also supplying `areaType` values that only exist under version '0'.
Supply `areaTypeVersion=0` explicitly if you want to restrict to current definitions only.

area.name: area
area.description:
6-character geographic area code, zero-padded on the left per the WID 3.0 structure specification (e.g., county FIPS 41005 → `041005`).
**The API normalizes area codes server-side**: supply the bare numeric code (e.g. `41005`) and the implementation will left-pad it with zeros to 6 characters.  Supplying the padded form (`041005`) also works.

periodType.name: periodType
periodType.description: Period type code. Common values: '01'=Annual, '03'=Monthly, '04'=Quarterly. Full list in `/lookups/periodTypes`.

period.name: period
period.description: Zero-padded period within the year (e.g. '06' for June for monthly data; '00' for annual records).

industryCodeType.name: industryCodeType
industryCodeType.description: Industry code classification type. Common values: '05'=SIC, '10'=NAICS 2022, '11'=NAICS 2017.

industryCode.name: industryCode
industryCode.description:
Industry classification code.  In the WID 3.0 database this is stored as a 10-character field, left-justified and right-padded with blanks (ASCII 32) per the WID 3.0 structure specification.
**The API normalizes codes server-side**: supply the bare code (e.g. `722511`) and the implementation will right-pad it to 10 characters before querying. Supplying the padded form (`722511    `) also works.

occupationCodeType.name: occupationCodeType
occupationCodeType.description: Occupation code classification type. Common values: '19'=SOC 2018, '14'=SOC 2010, '45'=OES wage occupations.

occupationCode.name: occupationCode
occupationCode.description:
Occupation classification code.  In the WID 3.0 database this is stored as a 10-character field, left-justified and right-padded with blanks (ASCII 32) per the WID 3.0 structure specification.
**The API normalizes codes server-side**: supply the bare code (e.g. `35-2014`) and the implementation will right-pad it to 10 characters before querying. Supplying the padded form (`35-2014   `) also works.

sort.name: sort
sort.description:
Comma-separated list of fields to sort by, each with an optional direction suffix (`:asc` or `:desc`).  Direction defaults to `asc` when omitted.
Examples:
  - `sort=periodYear:desc` — most-recent year first
  - `sort=periodYear:desc,period:asc` — descending year, then ascending period within year
  - `sort=stFips,areaType,area` — geographic sort, all ascending

Field names must match the response schema field names for the requested endpoint.  Unknown field names should return HTTP 400.

Metadata.description: Pagination and provenance metadata returned with every list response

widVersion.description: WID database version the data was drawn from

paginationMode.description: Pagination mode used for this response

cursor.description: Echo of the request cursor when cursor pagination is used

nextCursor.description: Cursor token for the next page when more records are available

dataSource.description: Identifier for the source state or system

dataAsOf.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) through which the returned data is current. Reflects the most recent load date for the tables contributing to this response.  `null` if the implementation does not track load dates.

ErrorResponse.description: Standard error envelope. The `errors` array may contain multiple entries when several validation problems are detected in a single request (e.g. two unknown parameter names).  The `status` field echoes the HTTP status code as a string, matching the HTTP response status.

status.description: HTTP status code as a string

detail.example: stfps is not a valid parameter name.

Geography.description: A geographic area descriptor from the WID 3.0 `Geographies` table. Primary key: (stFips, areaType, areaTypeVersion, area).

areaTypeVersion.description: Area type version — new in WID 3.0; default is '0'

stFips.description: 2-digit state FIPS code. `'00'` = United States (national data) — this record is included in the table for reference but is not a state.

stateName.description: Full state name. For `stFips = '00'` this is `'United States'`.

IndustryCode.description: An entry from the WID 3.0 `IndustryCodes` multi-code table

OccupationCode.description: An entry from the WID 3.0 `OccupationCodes` multi-code table

CesCode.description: An entry from the WID 3.0 `CESCodes` lookup table. `stFips` was added to this table in the May 2025 addenda.

stFips.description: State FIPS. Added May 2025 to accommodate definitional differences between state-level and national-level CES series codes.

seriesDesc.description: Description of the industries comprising the series

Ces.description: A single record from the WID 3.0 `CES` table. Contains employment, production-worker employment, hours, and earnings for one state/area/series/period combination. Suppress flags indicate which sub-measures must be withheld from public display.

empCES.description: Employment by place of work; actual count (not in thousands)

hoursPerWeek.description: Average hours per week for production workers

earningsPerWeek.description: Average weekly earnings for production workers

earningsPerHour.description: Average hourly earnings for production workers

hoursAllWorkers.description: Average hours per week for all workers

earningsAllWorkers.description: Average weekly earnings for all workers

LaborForce.description: A single record from the WID 3.0 `LaborForce` table (LAUS).

Industry.description: A single record from the WID 3.0 `Industry` table (ES-202/QCEW). Monthly employment fields (`month1Emp`, `month2Emp`, `month3Emp`) represent the three months of the reporting quarter, or months 1/2/3 for annual aggregates.

quarterAvgEmp.description: Average employment for the quarter or year

topEmployerAvgEmp.description: Average employment for the top employer in this geography/industry

Projection.description: A single record from the WID 3.0 `ProjectionsMatrix` table. Represents employment for one industry-occupation cell in a projection run.

periodYear.description: Base year of the projection run

pctEstInd.description: Occupation as % of industry employment (base year)

pctEstOcc.description: Industry as % of occupation employment (base year)

IndDirectory.description: A MicroMatrix industry code entry from `IndDirectories`

subTotal.description: Sum level indicator for the matrix industry

ownership.description: 2-digit ownership code for this projection industry entry. See `/lookups/ownerships` for valid values.

OccDirectory.description: A MicroMatrix occupation code entry from `OccDirectories`

MatrixXInd.description: Crosswalk from a matrix industry code to a standard IndustryCodes entry

MatrixXOcc.description: Crosswalk from a matrix occupation code to a standard OccupationCodes entry

Wage.description: A single record from the WID 3.0 `IOWage` table. Three separate suppress flags control whether wages, total record, or employment may be published.

experiencedWage.description: Mean of the upper two-thirds (ALC definition)

userDefinedRangeLoPct.description: Low end of user-defined wage range (percentile)

userDefinedRangeHiPct.description: High end of user-defined wage range (percentile)

userDefinedRangeMean.description: Mean wage within the user-defined percentile range

LicenseAuthority.description: A licensing authority from the WID 3.0 `LicenseAuthorities` table

License.description: An individual occupational license from the WID 3.0 `License` table

LicenseHistory.description: Count of licenses awarded from the WID 3.0 `LicenseHistory` table

LicenseXOcc.description: Crosswalk from a license to a standard OccupationCodes entry

CesWithGeography.description: CES record enriched with geography name and series title

LaborForceWithGeography.description: LaborForce record enriched with geography name

WageWithDescriptions.description: IOWage record enriched with occupation title, industry title, area name, and source descriptions

ProjectionWithTitles.description: ProjectionsMatrix record enriched with industry title, occupation title, area name, and growth code description

IndustryWithGeography.description: Industry QCEW record enriched with area name, industry title, and ownership name

LicensingByOccupation.description: Licensing requirement record from joining LicenseXOcc with License and LicenseAuthorities

TableStatusEntry.description: Data availability for one table/state combination

dataAsOf.description: ISO 8601 date (YYYY-MM-DD) through which data is current for this table and state.  `null` if the table has not been loaded for this state.

periodYearMin.description: Earliest period year present in this table for this state

periodYearMax.description: Most recent period year present in this table for this state

generatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when this status snapshot was generated

externalDocs.description: Find out more about the Workforce Information Database
